description:
## Purpose
New data layer function that reconstructs the full status journey for each issue from changelog data. This is the foundation for the Flow Analysis tab (bd-XXXX). No UI changes.

## Context from design session
The Flow Analysis page answers: 'what must we do to deliver faster?' by showing individual item paths through workflow statuses. This bead covers only the data computation — emitting a structured status path per item that the UI layer will consume.

The key insight from code analysis: _calculate_time_in_statuses() in data/flow_metrics_time.py already walks the changelog and computes dwell time per status group — but sums it and discards the per-stage breakdown. This bead extends that logic to emit a list instead of a sum.

## What to build

### New function: data/flow_analysis.py (new file)
Function signature:
    def get_issue_status_path(
        issue: dict,
        now: datetime | None = None,
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

Returns per-issue status path dict:
{
    'key': 'PROJ-123',
    'summary': 'Issue title',
    'issue_type': 'Story',
    'parent_key': 'PROJ-100',
    'parent_summary': 'Epic name',
    'current_status': 'Code Review',
    'is_completed': bool,
    'completed_at': datetime | None,
    'started_at': datetime | None,  # first WIP status entry
    'total_duration_days': float,   # start to now/completion
    'current_status_duration_days': float,  # days in current status (0 if completed)
    'longest_status': str,          # status name with most time
    'longest_status_days': float,
    'transition_count': int,        # total status changes (rework signal)
    'status_path': [                # ordered list, chronological
        {
            'status': 'In Progress',
            'entered_at': '2026-02-01T09:00:00Z',
            'exited_at': '2026-02-03T14:00:00Z',  # None if still current
            'duration_days': 2.2,
            'is_current': bool,
            'is_active_status': bool,   # from active_statuses config
            'is_wait_status': bool,     # WIP but not active
        }
    ]
}

### New function: data/flow_analysis.py
Function signature:
    def get_flow_analysis_data(
        issues: list[dict],
        flow_end_statuses: list[str],
        flow_wip_statuses: list[str],
        active_statuses: list[str],
        data_points_count: int = 12,
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

Returns:
{
    'open_items': [list of get_issue_status_path() dicts, open items only],
    'completed_items': [list of get_issue_status_path() dicts, completed within window],
    'summary': {
        'total_open': int,
        'total_completed': int,
        'avg_current_status_wait_days': float,  # open items only
        'avg_total_journey_days': float,         # completed items only
        'bottleneck_status': str,                # status with most total dwell time across all open items
        'bottleneck_status_item_count': int,
        'bottleneck_status_avg_days': float,
    }
}

### Population logic
- Open items: all issues in dataset where current status is NOT in flow_end_statuses, regardless of last activity timestamp. This intentionally catches silently stuck items (the most problematic) that have no recent changelog activity.
- Completed items: issues where current status IS in flow_end_statuses AND completed within data_points_count weeks.
- Status path starts from the FIRST transition into any flow_wip_statuses status (not from issue creation — pre-WIP time is excluded).
- For open items still in their current status, exited_at = None and duration_days is computed using now.

### Key code references
- data/flow_metrics_time.py: _calculate_time_in_statuses() — logic to adapt (emit list, not sum)
- data/flow_metrics_helpers.py: _find_first_transition_to_statuses() — reuse for started_at
- data/changelog_processor.py: calculate_time_in_status() — reference for transition walking
- data/active_work_manager.py: _add_health_indicators() — already computes days_since_status_change, is_blocked, is_aging — cross-reference but do NOT duplicate
- data/persistence/sqlite/changelog.py: get_changelog_entries() with field_name='status' — use for querying if needed, but issues from get_issues() already carry changelog in histories
- data/flow_metrics.py: _get_field_mappings() — use to load active_statuses, wip_statuses from settings

### Architecture rules
- This file belongs in data/ — pure business logic, no Dash imports
- All functions must have full type annotations
- Max function size 50 lines — split helpers as needed
- Use logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) pattern
- No emoji in logs or comments
- Parameterized only — no hardcoded status names

## Tests required
- tests/unit/data/test_flow_analysis.py
- Must use tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() for any file I/O
- Cover: item with no changelog (edge case), item that never entered WIP (edge case), item still in first status, item with multiple transitions, item that bounced between statuses (transition_count > stages), completed item, open item stuck silently
- Min 80% line coverage on data/flow_analysis.py
title: Flow Analysis: per-item status path computation

description:
When changing status mappings in Configure JIRA Mappings > Status tab (e.g., removing 'In Review' from Active statuses or 'Selected' from WIP statuses), Flow metrics do NOT update even after clicking 'Save Mappings', 'Update Data', or 'Force Refresh'.

REPRODUCTION:
1. Open Configure JIRA Mappings modal > Status tab
2. Remove a status from 'Active' or 'WIP' lists (e.g., remove 'In Review')
3. Click 'Save Mappings' (green toast confirms save)
4. Click 'Update Data' button
5. Check Flow Efficiency or Flow Load metric
6. ACTUAL: Metric value unchanged (e.g., Flow Load still 23)
7. EXPECTED: Metric recalculated with new mappings

TECHNICAL FINDINGS:
- Status mappings ARE saved correctly to database (verified)
- calculate_flow_efficiency() and calculate_flow_load() DO read from database dynamically (no hardcoding)
- ROOT CAUSE: Metrics cache optimization in data/metrics_calculator.py lines 124-147
- When metrics exist for historical weeks, system returns cached values WITHOUT checking if configuration changed
- New mappings never applied because recalculation is skipped

IMPACT:
- Critical UX bug: Users cannot adjust Flow metrics calculations
- Configuration changes appear to be ignored
- No way to see effect of status mapping changes without manual cache clearing

SOLUTION:
Option 1: Invalidate metrics cache when status mappings change (save_field_mappings callback)
Option 2: Add 'Recalculate Metrics' button that forces full recalculation ignoring cache
Option 3: Store config hash with each snapshot, recalculate if hash changed
notes:
COMPLETE FIX:

1. Root cause: In-memory snapshots cache not cleared on config save
   - Added clear_snapshots_cache() call after backend.delete_metrics()
   
2. UI reactivity: Metrics tabs didn't auto-update after save
   - Added metrics-refresh-trigger output to save_or_validate_mappings callback
   - Returns time.time() timestamp on successful save
   - Triggers Flow/DORA metrics callbacks to refresh immediately
   
RESULT: When user clicks 'Save Mappings':
1. Validates and saves config
2. Deletes metrics_data_points rows from database
3. Clears _snapshots_cache in-memory
4. Updates metrics-refresh-trigger store with timestamp
5. Flow/DORA tabs immediately show 'No Metrics' state (empty state component)
6. User clicks 'Update Data' → recalculates with new config → displays updated metrics

BEHAVIOR MATCHES: Same UX as 'Update Data' button - metrics tabs react immediately.
title: Flow metrics not updating when status mappings changed

description:
UI consistency inspection progress and findings:

Findings:
- Tooltips: width capped at 300px in ui/tooltip_utils.py (create_tooltip max_width) and assets/components/tooltips.css; additional tooltip styling exists in assets/components/visual-polish.css causing overrides. Tooltips feel too narrow and inconsistent.
- Toasts: ui/toast_notifications.py hard-codes minWidth=400px inline; toast polish exists in assets/components/visual-polish.css but width/spacing are not centralized.
- Alerts/messages: assets/components/alerts.css defines base alert styling, but ui/update_notification.py applies inline border/typography, creating drift.
- Inline styles in Python: hard-coded colors and typography in ui/work_distribution_card.py, ui/scope_metrics.py, callbacks/visualization_helpers/tab_content.py; should be replaced with design tokens or CSS classes.
- Empty states: custom empty-state markup/styles in callbacks/visualization_helpers/tab_content.py instead of standardized styles in assets/components/empty-state.css.
- Buttons: variants in assets/components/buttons.css with additional behavior in assets/components/visual-polish.css and action overrides in assets/components/long-press.css; needs consolidation to keep primary/secondary/outline consistent.
- Form tokens: assets/components/forms.css references --color-border-subtle and --color-surface; verify consistency in core variables to avoid drift.

Remediation plan (alignment + extraction):
- Tooltips: consolidate styling in one CSS source; increase width (e.g., 420-520px) and make responsive; stop setting max_width inline and use class-based width.
- Toasts: standardize sizing/spacing in CSS with a shared class; remove inline width from Python.
- Alerts/messages: replace inline styles with alert variants and shared classes; align update banners with alert system.
- Inline styles: extract repeated style dicts into CSS classes or shared helpers (colors, font sizes, empty states).
- Colors/icons: replace hex colors in UI code with design tokens/get_color to align semantics.
- Buttons/controls: consolidate button overrides; ensure consistent hover/focus/disabled states across views.
title: UI consistency inspection and alignment

description:
## Goal
Split three budget-domain files that exceed 1000 lines into focused sub-modules, following the established pattern from the P1 file-split beads (e.g. data/processing_*.py).

## Files to split

### 1. data/budget_calculator.py (1009L)
Scan the file with rg to identify logical sections. Likely split:
- data/budget_calculator_baseline.py -- baseline vs actual calculation (get_budget_baseline_vs_actual)
- data/budget_calculator_weekly.py   -- weekly budget series (get_budget_at_week, weekly helpers)
- data/budget_calculator_breakdown.py -- cost breakdown by type (calculate_cost_breakdown_by_type)
Keep data/budget_calculator.py as a re-export shim (same pattern as data/processing.py).

### 2. ui/budget_settings_card.py (1064L)
Likely split:
- ui/budget_settings_jira.py       -- JIRA-connected budget fields
- ui/budget_settings_thresholds.py -- threshold/alert configuration
- ui/budget_settings_costs.py      -- cost and rate input fields
Keep ui/budget_settings_card.py as a re-export shim.

### 3. ui/budget_cards/timeline_cards.py (978L)
Likely split:
- ui/budget_cards/timeline_cards_summary.py -- summary/header cards
- ui/budget_cards/timeline_cards_detail.py  -- detailed timeline breakdown cards
Keep ui/budget_cards/timeline_cards.py as a re-export shim.

## Process for each file
1. Read the entire file to understand all public functions/classes.
2. Group by responsibility. Each group becomes a new canonical module.
3. Create canonical modules with proper module docstrings.
4. Replace original file content with a re-export shim (see data/processing.py for exact shim pattern).
5. Run: python validate.py --fast
6. Run: python -m pytest tests/ -x -q --tb=short
7. Fix any import errors, re-run until clean.
8. Commit: refactor(arch): split oversized budget layer files (bd-XXXX)

## Rules
- Do NOT change any function signatures or logic -- behavior-preserving only.
- NOqa: F401 on all shim re-exports.
- Max 500 lines per new file.
- Update __all__ lists in shims.
title: Split oversized budget layer files (>900L)

close_reason: Implemented
description:
Feature goal
Add intelligent filtering/search on Active Work page using item summary and Jira standard fields.

User problem
Active Work currently shows all qualifying items. Users need quick narrowing by text and field filters, e.g. labels:Frontend,frontend, while preserving Jira case-sensitive behavior for labels.

Preliminary research (repo-based)
- Active Work render path: callbacks/active_work_timeline.py -> data/active_work_manager.py -> ui/active_work_epic_timeline.py.
- Existing issue storage already persists and returns fields needed for filtering:
  - labels (array)
  - fixVersions / fix_versions (array of objects)
  - components (array of objects)
  Source: data/persistence/sqlite/issues_crud.py.
- Jira field utility documents fixVersions as standard field mapping usage. Source: data/jira/field_utils.py.
- Existing autocomplete pattern available and mature:
  - UI input + suggestion container pattern ids in ui/field_mapping_modal.py.
  - Clientside dataset + filtering architecture in assets/namespace_autocomplete_clientside.js and callbacks/namespace_autocomplete.py.

Standard field verification
- labels: Jira standard field (array of strings).
- fixVersions: Jira standard field (array of version objects).
- components: Jira standard field (array of component objects).
- Repository handling aligns with these as first-class standard fields and not customfield_ ids.

Proposed UX (MVP)
- Add one compact search/filter panel at top of Active Work page.
- Single text input + Search button + Clear button.
- Trigger filter on Enter and Search click.
- Results update in-place on Active Work list/timeline.
- Filtering applies to child items and keeps parent rows visible when at least one child matches.

Proposed query language (v1)
- Free text token: matches issue key + summary (case-insensitive contains).
  Example: payment api
- Field filters:
  - labels:<v1,v2,...>
  - fixVersions:<v1,v2,...>
  - components:<v1,v2,...>
  - name:<text> (explicit summary filter; optional because free text already searches summary)
- Multi-value semantics within one field: OR.
  labels:Frontend,frontend => match either label.
- Across different fields/tokens: AND.
  labels:Frontend components:UI login => items must satisfy all clauses.
- Case policy:
  - labels: exact case-sensitive comparison (Jira-aligned per request).
  - fixVersions/components/name/free-text: case-insensitive contains by default for usability.
- Unknown field token: ignored with inline validation hint (non-blocking) in v1.

Autocomplete proposal
- Reuse existing field-mapping autocomplete interaction model:
  - Suggest field keys first: labels, fixVersions, components, name.
  - After typing field:, suggest distinct values seen in current Active Work dataset.
  - Keyboard support: up/down, Enter/Tab select, Esc dismiss.
- Smart templates:
  - Typing lab -> suggest labels:
  - Typing labels:Fr -> suggest labels:Frontend (exact value casing preserved)
- Data source for suggestions: current timeline dataset in memory (fast, local).

Technical design (no implementation yet)
1) UI layer (ui/)
- Add Active Work search panel component (input/button/clear/validation area).

2) Callbacks layer (callbacks/)
- Add callback wiring for submit action (Enter/button), clear action, query store, and rerender trigger.
- Keep callbacks thin; delegate parse/filter logic to data/.

3) Data layer (data/)
- Add parser module for filter language -> normalized AST/criteria.
- Add matcher module that evaluates issue + parent visibility rules.
- Integrate into active_work_manager pipeline after date/project filtering and before timeline grouping.

4) Assets (assets/)
- Add clientside autocomplete script for Active Work filter input, patterned after namespace autocomplete, but scoped to this panel.

Parent/child visibility rules
- If child matches -> show child and its parent group.
- If parent matches but no child matches:
  - Default v1 behavior: show parent with no child rows hidden (or no-match state under parent).
  - Alternative to decide during implementation: include all children when parent matches by name.
- If no items match -> show explicit empty filtered state with clear CTA.

Acceptance criteria (for implementation follow-up)
1. Entering labels:Frontend,frontend filters Active Work to items whose labels include exactly Frontend OR frontend and displays relevant parents.
2. Enter key and Search button behave identically.
3. Clear button resets query and restores original Active Work list.
4. Free text matches issue key and summary.
5. Field filters supported: labels, fixVersions, components, name.
6. Autocomplete suggests fields and values from current dataset and supports keyboard selection.
7. Invalid tokens do not crash; user gets lightweight hint.
8. Performance remains responsive with current Active Work volumes (target <100ms for typical interactions).

Open decisions for implementation
- Parent-only match behavior (expand all children vs parent-only row).
- Whether fixVersions/components comparison should be exact vs contains in v1.
- Whether to preserve filters across tab switches.

Out of scope (this bead)
- No code changes.
- No UI implementation.
- No parser/filter implementation.
- No tests.

Implementation decomposition suggestion
- Could be split into 2 beads:
  A) Parser + filtering engine + tests
  B) UI panel + callbacks + autocomplete integration
- Or keep single feature bead.
title: Active Work intelligent filtering and search
